task_completion.description: Open the patient record for Roberto Carlos and initiate a clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GNU Health window displaying the patient 'Roberto Carlos' with a clinical evaluation form open or saved.

task_completion.description: Document respiratory distress and elevated heart rate in the clinical evaluation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text fields in the clinical evaluation mentioning 'respiratory distress', 'shortness of breath', 'elevated heart rate', or a high pulse rate entered in the vitals section.

task_completion.description: Add a toxicological inhalation diagnosis (ICD-10 T59).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A diagnosis record associated with the evaluation showing an ICD-10 code starting with 'T59' (e.g., Toxic effect of other gases, fumes and vapors).
A prescription record for the patient containing 'Salbutamol', 'Albuterol', 'Ipratropium', or 'Levalbuterol'.

task_completion.description: Order at least two baseline laboratory tests.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lab requests section showing at least two distinct tests ordered (e.g., toxicology screen, metabolic panel, blood gases).

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 1 to 5 days.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment record for Roberto Carlos with a date set 1 to 5 days in the future.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical interface to complete the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the GNU Health desktop client, not terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The records are created within the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Forms, prescriptions, and appointments are visible within the standard GNU Health UI windows, not typed into a text editor or separate document.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No database clients (like psql) or terminal windows executing SQL queries are visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient is Roberto Carlos. The diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'T59'. The bronchodilator prescription must match one of: Salbutamol, Albuterol, Ipratropium, or Levalbuterol. The agent must order at least 2 lab tests. The follow-up appointment must be scheduled 1 to 5 days after the current date.
